response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with strong web and full-stack credentials, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ experience is a seven-month stint at National Instruments ending in 2015, C++ is absent from his skills list, and he shows no exposure to MQTT, IEC 62443, SDL practices, or industrial protocols. His low-latency DLang work is transferable in spirit, but the core technical match is too weak for a senior C++-focused position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: Professional C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; C++ is not listed among current skills
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, MQTT, or fieldbus/OT protocol experience
Recent career is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js) — a significant domain shift from networked multi-threaded C++ platform development

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IoT protocol exposure
